name: Barrakuda FS
description: Sandboxed local filesystem access — list/read/search/edit/write/delete inside a confined root, plus user-approved grants for folders outside it.
author: Tentaculum-team

tools.name: fs.list
tools.description: [fsRead] Lists a directory on the LOCAL machine. Path rules for ALL fs.* tools: `path` is relative to the sandbox root (default "."), or an ABSOLUTE path anywhere else — first use outside the sandbox asks the user to approve inline (blocks until answered; persists after). Read-only.

path.description: Directory to list. Defaults to "." (the sandbox root).

tools.name: fs.read
tools.description: [fsRead] Reads a text file (UTF-8; binary is refused), each line prefixed `N→` (1-indexed). Returns up to `limit` lines starting at `offset`; a truncated result says which offset to continue from — read only the range you need. Path rules: see fs.list. Read-only.

max_bytes.description: Max bytes read from disk (default 10MB, ceiling 50MB). Rarely needed — use offset/limit instead.

tools.name: fs.edit
tools.description: [fsWrite] Replaces old_string with new_string inside a text file. old_string must match the CURRENT content exactly once, unless replace_all is true — 0 or 2+ matches are refused. Plain find/replace, not a patch: use fs.read first and copy the exact text. Path rules: see fs.list. Mutates the filesystem.

old_string.description: Exact text to find. Must match exactly once unless replace_all is true.

new_string.description: Exact replacement text. May be empty to delete old_string.

tools.name: fs.search
tools.description: [fsRead] Recursive regex search across text files starting at `path`. Returns matches as `path:line: text`, capped at `max_results` (default 200, ceiling 1000; hitting the cap is flagged). Binary/oversized files are skipped. Path rules: see fs.list. Read-only.

pattern.description: Regular expression, matched line by line.

path.description: Directory (or file) to search. Defaults to "." (the sandbox root).

tools.name: fs.glob
tools.description: [fsRead] Finds files by NAME pattern recursively, starting at `path` (default "." = the sandbox root). This matches file NAMES, not content — use fs.search for content. `pattern` is shell-glob syntax (`*`, `?`, `[...]`), not regex: `"*.go"` matches at any depth by base name; a pattern containing `/` matches against the path relative to `path` instead. Path rules: see fs.list. Read-only.

pattern.description: Shell-glob pattern to match file names against (e.g. "*.go", "internal/*.go"). Not regex.

path.description: Directory to search under. Defaults to "." (the sandbox root).

max_results.description: Max matching paths to return (default 200, hard ceiling 1000).

tools.name: fs.write
tools.description: [fsWrite] Creates or fully overwrites a text file. Content over 10MB is refused. `create_dirs` (default true) creates missing parents. Path rules: see fs.list. Mutates the filesystem.

path.description: File to write. Overwritten if it exists.

tools.name: fs.delete
tools.description: [fsWrite] Deletes a file or EMPTY directory — never recursive, and deleting the sandbox root or a granted folder is refused. Path rules: see fs.list. Mutates the filesystem.

path.description: File or empty directory to delete.

tools.name: fs.request_access
tools.description: [fsWrite] OPTIONAL pre-authorization: asks the USER for access to a folder outside the sandbox without performing an operation. Every other fs.* tool already does this itself on first use of an absolute path — call this only to secure access ahead of time, or to check access without touching anything. If approved, all fs.* tools can use ABSOLUTE paths under it and the grant persists across restarts. If declined, do not retry without new instructions. Blocks until answered.

path.description: Absolute path of an EXISTING directory.

tools.name: fs.known_dirs
tools.description: [fsRead] Lists absolute paths of well-known user folders (home, Desktop, Documents, Downloads) plus the sandbox root — paths only, no contents. Use to find a real path, then call the fs.* tool you need with it directly. No parameters.
